questions.text: When I witness something moving, my eyes well up before I've even decided how I feel about it.
I rarely detect when a person's generosity has a strategic element to it.
I pick up on when someone is narrating an event with a particular audience member in mind.
I find that the thought of keeping something for myself while others lack is deeply uncomfortable.
I prefer to move fast and fix errors when they surface rather than reviewing obsessively.
I notice that the anticipation of seeing someone I've missed builds to a physical crescendo in my chest.
I feel that my organizational systems give me a sense of calm and mastery.
I find that I miss unspoken social expectations that others seem to just know.
I am the one who usually proposes the plan for a group activity.
I detect the social undercurrents at a family gathering that newcomers would miss entirely.
I tend not to think about why someone chose to sit where they sat.
I hold the view that the moral concern I feel for suffering people is the experience that most directly informs my sense of what a good life requires.
I sometimes feel like a lightning rod for emotional intensity.
I tend to check with the group before escalating any issue.
I believe that the ability to evaluate competing claims of need without emotional favoritism is essential for fair resource distribution.
I find that my emotional baseline recovers so quickly that others sometimes assume I wasn't affected.
I feel that the moral pull of another person's suffering is among the most trustworthy signals I can follow.
I experience nostalgia as a pleasant memory rather than an aching longing.
I believe that approaching others' pain with structured reasoning allows me to be more consistently helpful over time.
I sense when someone is restraining themselves from saying what they really think.
I find that I'm able to remain present and attentive even in emotionally charged conversations.
I am the type of person who walks the roadmap alongside others.
I find that I am better positioned to help when I can evaluate a situation without being influenced by the emotions it generates in me.
I routinely block off calendar time that belongs exclusively to me.
I enjoy the challenge of persuading others to follow my direction.
I feel that the moral demand of suffering is the consideration that most decisively separates what I should do from what is merely convenient.
I note important dates and send reminders to myself well in advance.
I use deliberate practice techniques when building a new skill.
I find that I am most engaged when working on something that directly serves my vision.
I hold the view that the person most responsible for my well-being is me.
I feel most at peace when I know I have put others before myself.
I finalize plans late because earlier decisions might need changing anyway.
My sleep schedule shifts depending on what I'm engaged with; I follow my energy.
I prefer when someone else organizes the agenda and I can support their plan.
I tend to be the voice that names the elephant in the room.
I believe I have an obligation to contribute more than I consume.
